body.title: [dependency] Availability semaphore encodes build CLASS/level, not a bare .true touch-file
body.intention: I intend that a worker's availability semaphore carries WHICH build class/level it is — so the orchestrator can tell 'a builder exists but not of sufficient level' apart from 'no builder at all', instead of treating availability as a bare present/absent boolean.
body.description:
PHASE 2 (of D-granny-pull-dispatch-model). Reconciliation verdict ABSENT: the flag is a bare presence touch-file (DickSimnelShim._write_available writes 'true' shim.py:62; CC _mark_available .touch() worker_shim.py:230-234; is_available only tests .exists() availability.py:40-46). Encode the build class/level in the semaphore (in the filename or contents) and teach the availability read to return class/level, so 'found-but-insufficient-level' becomes expressible. Couples with T-converge-worker-dispatch (the capability-tag rules engine consumes this) — gate on it so the tag/level vocabulary is settled first.

**Affected files:** unseen_university/devices/granny/availability.py (read/parse class), unseen_university/devices/dicksimnel/shim.py + frontdoor.py (_write_available), unseen_university/devices/claude/worker_shim.py (_mark_available).
**Design rules:** log-every-state-change (availability write is a state change); no-sqlite (flat-file semaphore); external-state (class lives in the flag, not memory).
**Scope boundary:** IN — encode + read build class/level in the availability semaphore across DS + CC shims. OUT — the routing decision that USES the level (T-converge), the alarm's use of insufficient-level (T-no-builders-alarm-done-right).
**Test plan:** availability read returns the worker's class/level (red: only bool; green: class present); a lower-level worker is distinguishable from absent (red: both read 'unavailable'; green: 'present-insufficient' vs 'absent').
**Completion criteria:** the availability semaphore carries build class/level and the availability API returns it; 'insufficient level' is distinguishable from 'absent'.
**Rollback:** revert to presence-only .true; readers fall back to boolean.
